operations.name: RawData+opencog-ml+Befuddling bug working with LG dictionary (14fe8a5ac55841d7)
operations.content:
OpenCog Mailing List Thread: Befuddling bug working with LG dictionary
Messages: 6
Date range: 2015-09-19 to 2015-09-24
Source: opencog@googlegroups.com
Thread ID: 14fe8a5ac55841d7

============================================================

--- Message 1/6 ---
From: Gavriel Loria
Date: 2015-09-19

By running my function wg-list, I appear to be changing what lg-get-dict-entry returns for a given WordNode. I am pretty confused how this could be.

--- Message 2/6 ---
From: Linas Vepstas
Date: 2015-09-23

What you are seeing is a rather wonky behavior. It returns all connector sets for a word; since you added a new one without deleting the old one you get all. Some words appear in the LG dict as nouns and as verbs; thus you get multiple sets. When you create the new definition, you should forcibly delete the old one.

--- Message 3/6 ---
From: William Ma
Date: 2015-09-23

You should not use LgWordCset to temporary store the connector. What you added is just a subset. Use a different atom type to wrap them. Otherwise you are just corrupting the disjuncts list.

--- Message 4/6 ---
From: Gavriel Loria
Date: 2015-09-24

Do you have simple instructions for creating a new atom type?

--- Message 5/6 ---
From: Linas Vepstas
Date: 2015-09-24

Edit this file: /opencog/nlp/types/atom_types.script add stuff to the bottom, and recompile and reinstall. It should all just work.

--- Message 6/6 ---
From: Linas Vepstas
Date: 2015-09-24

Also, please be aware that conceptually MyNewLinkType with Atoms is equivalent to an EvaluationLink with PredicateNode MyNewLinkType and ListLink. The first is less verbose but means the same thing.

operations.name: RawData+opencog-ml+Binding language inter-operation (14fda3824d23f61c)
operations.content:
OpenCog Mailing List Thread: Binding language inter-operation
Messages: 2
Date range: 2015-09-17 to 2015-09-17
Source: opencog@googlegroups.com
Thread ID: 14fda3824d23f61c

============================================================

--- Message 1/2 ---
From: 'Nil Geisweiller' via opencog
Date: 2015-09-17

Hi, I'd like to 1. Load PLN rules coded in Scheme in the AtomSpace, 2. Play with them in Haskell. Is there a way to do that today without using the cogserver?

Nil

--- Message 2/2 ---
From: Linas Vepstas
Date: 2015-09-17

Easiest way to solve this would be to write a haskell wrapper for the scheme eval function, and just call that. Just wrap the C++ eval_scheme function in haskell. Then you'd say: scheme_eval("(load-from-path \"pln.rule.whatever\")")

Providing the inverse would be cool too, allowing arbitrary haskell code to be invoked from scheme.

--linas

operations.name: RawData+opencog-ml+Building OpenCog (14c395a384122279)
operations.content:
OpenCog Mailing List Thread: Building OpenCog
Messages: 6
Date range: 2015-03-20 to 2015-04-23
Source: opencog@googlegroups.com
Thread ID: 14c395a384122279

============================================================

--- Message 1/6 ---
From: Tim Schwab
Date: 2015-03-20

I installed Ubuntu 14.04 fresh. The install-dependencies-trusty script fails with unmet dependencies for libcheese-gtk23 and libclutter. Universe repositories are enabled.

--- Message 2/6 ---
From: Linas Vepstas
Date: 2015-03-20

After installing ubuntu, you should say apt-get update then apt-get upgrade. These errors have nothing to do with opencog. Try uninstalling gstreamer1.0-clutter or libcheese-gtk23 and then run apt-get -f a few times. I am guessing some archaic version of cheese is screwing things up. Last time I was bit, it was by nvidia.

--- Message 3/6 ---
From: Tim Schwab
Date: 2015-03-20

Yeah, I had done that as well. I did it again just to make sure, and got the same error message.

--- Message 4/6 ---
From: Linas Vepstas
Date: 2015-03-21

Did you enable universe as the error message suggests? These errors only occur for a few rare reasons: a badly built package, or a package maintainer mistake. Try uninstalling the problematic packages.

--- Message 5/6 ---
From: Tim Schwab
Date: 2015-03-21

Thanks so much, I was able to download the dependencies and build it after I removed both of those.

--- Message 6/6 ---
From: Felix Velazquez
Date: 2015-04-23

It also worked for me after installing the NVIDIA drivers for my graphics card, so I guess that is the right explanation.

operations.name: RawData+opencog-ml+Build Errors (14ddea61d8d026fe)
operations.content:
OpenCog Mailing List Thread: Build Errors
Messages: 13
Date range: 10 Jun 2015 to 13 Jun 2015
Source: opencog@googlegroups.com
Thread ID: 14ddea61d8d026fe

============================================================

--- Message 1/13 ---
From: Roman Treutlein
Date: 10 Jun 2015

Hello,

i am currently trying to build OpenCog with the goal of eventually creating 
AUR (Arch User Repository) Packages.

While building Moses I had 2 Errors where the != Operater had clashing 
overloads defined in .../boost/variant.hpp and 
.../moses/moses/comboreduct/combo/vertex.h

 [...]

--- Message 2/13 ---
From: 'Nil Geisweiller' via opencog
Date: 2015-06-11

Hi,

I have boost 1.54, I'm unable to reproduce that, could be a too new 
version of boost indeed. Note that you don't need to compile MOSES in 
order to compile/use opencog. The subset that depends on MOSES would 
simply not be compiled.

Nil

--- Message 3/13 ---
From: Roman Treutlein
Date: 11 Jun 2015

Hey,

yea that seems to have fixed that error. Also now i get the following:

../libcomboreduct.so: undefined reference to 
`opencog::combo::operator>>(std::istream&, 
opencog::tree<boost::variant<double, opencog::combo::enum_t, 
opencog::combo::id::builtin, opencog::combo::id::wild_card, 
 [...]

--- Message 4/13 ---
From: Roman Treutlein
Date: 11 Jun 2015

Okay got it to work fine on Ubuntu.

I was trying test the unity3d World a bit but unfortunately it fails with:

"[ERROR] OAC is not available, dying gracefully."

This is the output on the Embodiment Side after connecting.

ZeroMQ publish end point is: tcp://127.0.0.1:18002
std::exception
 [...]

--- Message 5/13 ---
From: Linas Vepstas
Date: 2015-06-11

On Thu, Jun 11, 2015 at 6:45 AM, Roman Treutlein <lordmord94@gmail.com>
wrote:

Don't know.  is cxxtest version 4 not compatible with version 3? Anyway,
all the various compatibility bugs should be tracked down and fixed.

--linas

--- Message 6/13 ---
From: Roman Treutlein
Date: 11 Jun 2015

Well in the readme files it says that opencog requires version 3 and is not 
compatible with version 4.

I guess that is just outdated since it seems to work fine with version 4.

if you want i can create a pull requests with the readmes changed. This 
 [...]

--- Message 7/13 ---
From: Linas Vepstas
Date: 2015-06-11

If it actually works, then, yes, I guess the readme's are out of date.
Yes, please send a pull request!

I have this:

$ cxxtestgen --version
This is CxxTest version 4.3.

and it works for me...

--linas

On Thu, Jun 11, 2015 at 3:09 PM, Roman Treutlein <lordmord94@gmail.com>
wrote:

--- Message 8/13 ---
From: Roman Treutlein
Date: 11 Jun 2015

Yea in my fresh install i also have version 4.3

Pull requests sent.

regards
/roman

--- Message 9/13 ---
From: Amen Belayneh
Date: 11 Jun 2015

Hi,

have you 
tried https://github.com/opencog/opencog/blob/master/scripts/install-archlinux-dependencies.sh?

--- Message 10/13 ---
From: Roman Treutlein
Date: 12 Jun 2015

Hi Amen,
 

yes and it does install most of the dependencies but fails with some. In 
the end my build problems remain. Ill probably try to work it out next week.
Also the script seems to be meant for 32bit systems and not 64bit which is 
what i am using..

regards
/Roman

--- Message 11/13 ---
From: Amen Belayneh
Date: 13 Jun 2015

actually I tried it in a docker container which runs only on 64 bit system. 
What made you think it is for 32 bit sytem?

--- Message 12/13 ---
From: Roman Treutlein
Date: 13 Jun 2015

The script tried to install gcc from multilib meaning the 32bit version 
which clashed with the 64bit version i already have installed. Also it says 
something about 32bit in the Comments of the Script.

--- Message 13/13 ---
From: Amen Belayneh
Date: 13 Jun 2015

Oh yap

If you have any improvements that will make it work seamlessly on both 32 
and 64 bit systems please don't hesitate to make a pull request. 

Thanks:-)

operations.name: RawData+opencog-ml+cog-get-pred examples or documentation (14b84866348901dd)
operations.content:
OpenCog Mailing List Thread: cog-get-pred examples or documentation
Messages: 7
Date range: 2015-02-13 to 2015-02-17
Source: opencog@googlegroups.com
Thread ID: 14b84866348901dd

============================================================

--- Message 1/7 ---
From: Jim Rutt
Date: 2015-02-13

The cog-get-pred scheme procedure sounds useful but the description doesn't provide much on how to use it.

--- Message 2/7 ---
From: Linas Vepstas
Date: 2015-02-13

I just pushed a fix enabling documentation for all functions in utilities.scm. cog-get-pred returns a list of predicates of the given type that an instance participates in via EvaluationLinks.

--- Message 3/7 ---
From: Jim Rutt
Date: 2015-02-17

Docs are helpful, but calling them up from the guile prompt doesn't seem to work, I get a syntax error.

--- Message 4/7 ---
From: Jim Rutt
Date: 2015-02-17

Also, the documentation isn't quite clear enough. cog-get-pred with a specific PredicateNode returns an empty list.

--- Message 5/7 ---
From: Linas Vepstas
Date: 2015-02-17

The shell at port 17001 does not support the meta-commands, such as ,describe. You would have to use either the repl shell at 18001 or use guile directly.

--- Message 6/7 ---
From: Linas Vepstas
Date: 2015-02-17

The intended use is: (cog-get-pred (ConceptNode "Dog") 'PredicateNode). Most utilities are very short, typically 5-10 lines of code, and documentation tends to exceed the code length.

--- Message 7/7 ---
From: Jim Rutt
Date: 2015-02-17

Ok, that worked. Close to enough to what I need at this time.

operations.name: RawData+opencog-ml+Concerning 'Opencog for Noobs' (14ea53d3b1070a3f)
operations.content:
OpenCog Mailing List Thread: Concerning 'Opencog for Noobs'
Messages: 3
Date range: 19 Jul 2015 to 2015-07-19
Source: opencog@googlegroups.com
Thread ID: 14ea53d3b1070a3f

============================================================

--- Message 1/3 ---
From: Daniel Selvon
Date: 19 Jul 2015

The tutorial helps you compile opencog but once the server is up, what next? How do I send information to opencog? How do I program it to process information the way I want?

--- Message 2/3 ---
From: Linas Vepstas
Date: 2015-07-19

Probably the most interesting thing to do with opencog in the near future will be to play with the chatbot. However, it is currently almost non-functional. We hope to hook it up to a virtual hanson robotics head.

There is no way to have it automagically crunch weather data. There is a genetic program learner, but its never been used on extended spatial data.

--linas

--- Message 3/3 ---
From: Linas Vepstas
Date: 2015-07-19

To use moses, you'd have to organize your data into tabular form: lots of rows, lots of columns, trying to predict one column as a function of the others. Moses works well and has been applied in various places, including medical data: predicting patient behavior based on unstructured text.

PLN is something completely different; it is a way of making probabilistic logical inferences.

--linas

operations.name: RawData+opencog-ml+Continuing work on the Simplified Word Grammar Parser (14f49c4acbfd3f7c)
operations.content:
OpenCog Mailing List Thread: Continuing work on the Simplified Word Grammar Parser
Messages: 2
Date range: 19 Aug 2015 to 2015-08-20
Source: opencog@googlegroups.com
Thread ID: 14f49c4acbfd3f7c

============================================================

--- Message 1/2 ---
From: Gavriel Loria
Date: 19 Aug 2015

Hi, I am writing this post as an update about my GSoC project. I'm currently fixing some last bugs parsing artificially created sentences before moving on to testing real sentences. [...]

--- Message 2/2 ---
From: Linas Vepstas
Date: 2015-08-20

You should always be pushing your latest code to your github repo. If nothing else, it serves as a backup copy, so that you don't lose the code if your system crashes, your laptop gets stolen, etc. It also gives others a chance to review, discuss, etc.

--linas

operations.name: RawData+opencog-ml+Contirbuting to Opencog - December 2015 (151677ede3b9359a)
operations.content:
OpenCog Mailing List Thread: Contirbuting to Opencog - December 2015
Messages: 4
Date range: 2015-12-03 to 2015-12-04
Source: opencog@googlegroups.com
Thread ID: 151677ede3b9359a

============================================================

--- Message 1/4 ---
From: Nitesh Bhutani
Date: 2015-12-03

I am a recent graduate interested in AI. I have installed opencog and started playing with Python bindings. I am interested in MOSES for Reinforcement Learning, DeSTIN redesign, and Deep Learning perception.

--- Message 2/4 ---
From: Ben Goertzel
Date: 2015-12-03

It would be great to have you work on MOSES RL but now the best thing would be to do this in Minecraft not Unity3D, we have Minecraft hooks for OpenCog now. Nil Geisweiller can guide you. DeSTIN Redesign is pretty much obsolete. I am in the middle of writing a document clarifying Deep Learning perception.

--- Message 3/4 ---
From: 'Nil Geisweiller' via opencog
Date: 2015-12-03

That is an ambitious project that would require you to study MOSES and the embodiment code in depth. I am not very familiar with the new embodiment code, but I can certainly guide you through MOSES. Feel free to ping me on Slack.

--- Message 4/4 ---
From: Ben Goertzel
Date: 2015-12-04

Embodiment is reimplemented now, and the new version is what is used by the Minecraft bridge. A lot may be doable in deep learning via connecting existing python deep learning libraries to OpenCog appropriately.

operations.name: RawData+opencog-ml+Contributing to MOSES and the embodiment module (14c22297f0d52579)
operations.content:
OpenCog Mailing List Thread: Contributing to MOSES and the embodiment module
Messages: 2
Date range: 13 Mar 2015 to 2015-03-16
Source: opencog@googlegroups.com
Thread ID: 14c22297f0d52579

============================================================

--- Message 1/2 ---
From: Tapiawala Sahil
Date: 13 Mar 2015

I understood why the need for the shift from 3dspaceMaps to octomaps, but am having no headway with its implementation in code. Could you please guide me? Also Could you please elaborate on the scope of improving the hBOA algorithm?

--- Message 2/2 ---
From: Linas Vepstas
Date: 2015-03-16

Thanks! I am hoping that perhaps Nil can provide a more detailed response...

The best way to understand what is needed for the 3d/octo space map is to understand how it is going to be used. Unfortunately, I don't know this myself...

the hBOA problem is very different, and requires understanding how MOSES works.

--linas
